strategies.name: Interleave group storing zero-extended values
strategies.target: A loop with an interleave group (e.g., storing to an array of structs) where the stored values are zero-extended from a loaded interleave group.
strategies.rationale: This directly tests the addition of `VPWidenCastRecipe` support for `zext` in `narrowInterleaveGroups`.
strategies.expected_issue: Without the patch, the interleave group would not be narrowed, potentially leading to suboptimal code or crashes if the compiler expects it to be narrowed. With the patch, it should be narrowed successfully.

strategies.name: Interleave group storing sign-extended values
strategies.target: A loop with an interleave group where the stored values are sign-extended from a loaded interleave group.
strategies.expected_issue: Ensures `sext` is handled correctly during narrowing.

strategies.name: Interleave group storing truncated values
strategies.target: A loop with an interleave group where the stored values are truncated from a loaded interleave group.
strategies.expected_issue: Ensures `trunc` is handled correctly during narrowing.

strategies.name: Interleave group storing floating-point casted values
strategies.target: A loop with an interleave group where the stored values are casted using `fpext` or `fptrunc`.
strategies.rationale: Tests `VPWidenCastRecipe` support for floating-point casts like `fpext` or `fptrunc`.
strategies.expected_issue: Ensures FP casts are handled correctly during narrowing.

strategies.name: Mixed casts in interleave group
strategies.target: A loop with an interleave group where different members are casted using different cast operations.
strategies.rationale: `canNarrowOps` checks if all ops have the same opcode. If they have different opcodes (e.g., one `zext`, one `sext`), it should return false and not narrow.
strategies.expected_issue: If the check is flawed, it might try to narrow and crash or generate incorrect code. It should correctly identify that they cannot be narrowed together.

reason_thou:
### 1. Fix Understanding
The patch extends the `narrowInterleaveGroups` transformation in VPlan to support `VPWidenCastRecipe` in addition to `VPWidenRecipe`.
Before the patch, `canNarrowOps` and `narrowInterleaveGroupOp` only checked for `VPWidenRecipe`. If an interleave group's stored values were defined by a `VPWidenCastRecipe`, the narrowing transformation would fail or assert.
The patch changes `dyn_cast<VPWidenRecipe>` to `dyn_cast<VPSingleDefRecipe>` and checks `isa<VPWidenRecipe, VPWidenCastRecipe>(V)`.
It also updates `canNarrowLoad` to take `VPSingleDefRecipe` instead of `VPWidenRecipe`.

### 2. Formal Semantic Modeling
Before the fix:
`canNarrowOps` checks if all ops are `VPWidenRecipe` with the same opcode.
`narrowInterleaveGroupOp` narrows `VPWidenRecipe` by recursively narrowing its operands.

After the fix:
`canNarrowOps` checks if all ops are either `VPWidenRecipe` or `VPWidenCastRecipe` and have the same opcode/intrinsic ID.
`narrowInterleaveGroupOp` narrows both `VPWidenRecipe` and `VPWidenCastRecipe` by recursively narrowing their operands.

Conditions where they differ:
When an interleave group stores values that are the result of a cast operation (e.g., `zext`, `sext`, `trunc`) on a widened load or another narrowable operation.

### 3. Assumptions and Preconditions Identified
1. The operands of `VPWidenCastRecipe` can be narrowed correctly.
2. The opcode of `VPWidenCastRecipe` is correctly retrieved by `getOpcodeOrIntrinsicID`.
3. The narrowing of `VPWidenCastRecipe` does not violate any type constraints or assumptions in VPlan.

### 4. Potential Cases to Break Assumptions
1. A `VPWidenCastRecipe` with multiple operands (though casts usually have one, maybe some edge cases?).
2. A mix of `VPWidenRecipe` and `VPWidenCastRecipe` with the same opcode (unlikely since opcodes are distinct, but `getOpcodeOrIntrinsicID` might return the same for some?).
3. Narrowing a cast that feeds an interleave group where the cast itself is fed by a complex recipe that cannot be narrowed.

{"found": false, "tool": "difftest", "action": "test", "args": "-passes=loop-vectorize -force-vector-interleave=1 -force-vector-width=4 -enable-interleaved-mem-accesses -S", "original_ir": "
define void @pr128062(ptr %dst.start, i8 %a, i16 %b) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %dst = phi ptr [ %dst.start, %entry ], [ %dst.next, %loop ]
  %dst.next = getelementptr inbounds nuw i8, ptr %dst, i64 4
  %load.dst = load i8, ptr %dst, align 1
  %dst.ext = zext i8 %load.dst to i16
  %mul.dst.0 = mul nuw i16 %dst.ext, %b
  %udiv.0 = udiv i16 %mul.dst.0, 255
  %trunc.0 = trunc nuw i16 %udiv.0 to i8
  %val.0 = add i8 %a, %trunc.0
  store i8 %val.0, ptr %dst, align 1
  %gep.dst.1 = getelementptr inbounds nuw i8, ptr %dst, i64 1
  %load.dst.1 = load i8, ptr %gep.dst.1, align 1
  %dst.1.ext = zext i8 %load.dst.1 to i16
  %mul.dst.1 = mul nuw i16 %dst.1.ext, %b
  %udiv.1 = udiv i16 %mul.dst.1, 255
  %trunc.1 = trunc nuw i16 %udiv.1 to i8
  %val.1 = add i8 %a, %trunc.1
  store i8 %val.1, ptr %gep.dst.1, align 1
  %gep.dst.2 = getelementptr inbounds nuw i8, ptr %dst, i64 2
  %load.dst.2 = load i8, ptr %gep.dst.2, align 1
  %dst.2.ext = zext i8 %load.dst.2 to i16
  %mul.dst.2 = mul nuw i16 %dst.2.ext, %b
  %udiv.2 = udiv i16 %mul.dst.2, 255
  %trunc.2 = trunc nuw i16 %udiv.2 to i8
  %val.2 = add i8 %a, %trunc.2
  store i8 %val.2, ptr %gep.dst.2, align 1
  %gep.dst.3 = getelementptr inbounds nuw i8, ptr %dst, i64 3
  %load.dst.3 = load i8, ptr %gep.dst.3, align 1
  %dst.3.ext = zext i8 %load.dst.3 to i16
  %mul.dst.3 = mul nuw i16 %dst.3.ext, %b
  %udiv.3 = udiv i16 %mul.dst.3, 255
  %trunc.3 = trunc nuw i16 %udiv.3 to i8
  %val.3 = add i8 %a, %trunc.3
  store i8 %val.3, ptr %gep.dst.3, align 1
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 4
  %exit.cond = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 256
  br i1 %exit.cond, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

define void @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:
  call void @pr128062(ptr %dst.start, i8 1, i16 2)
  ret void
}

; ModuleID = '/data/tmp/tmp3x9hz7vz/orig.ll'
source_filename = "/data/tmp/tmp3x9hz7vz/orig.ll"

define void @pr128062(ptr %dst.start, i8 %a, i16 %b) {
entry:
  br label %vector.ph

vector.ph:                                        ; preds = %entry
  %broadcast.splatinsert = insertelement <4 x i16> poison, i16 %b, i64 0
  %broadcast.splat = shufflevector <4 x i16> %broadcast.splatinsert, <4 x i16> poison, <4 x i32> zeroinitializer
  %broadcast.splatinsert1 = insertelement <4 x i8> poison, i8 %a, i64 0
  %broadcast.splat2 = shufflevector <4 x i8> %broadcast.splatinsert1, <4 x i8> poison, <4 x i32> zeroinitializer
  br label %vector.body

vector.body:                                      ; preds = %vector.body, %vector.ph
  %index = phi i64 [ 0, %vector.ph ], [ %index.next, %vector.body ]
  %offset.idx = mul i64 %index, 4
  %next.gep = getelementptr i8, ptr %dst.start, i64 %offset.idx
  %wide.load = load <4 x i8>, ptr %next.gep, align 1
  %0 = zext <4 x i8> %wide.load to <4 x i16>
  %1 = mul nuw <4 x i16> %0, %broadcast.splat
  %2 = udiv <4 x i16> %1, splat (i16 255)
  %3 = trunc nuw <4 x i16> %2 to <4 x i8>
  %4 = add <4 x i8> %broadcast.splat2, %3
  store <4 x i8> %4, ptr %next.gep, align 1
  %index.next = add nuw i64 %index, 1
  %5 = icmp eq i64 %index.next, 64
  br i1 %5, label %middle.block, label %vector.body, !llvm.loop !0

middle.block:                                     ; preds = %vector.body
  br label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %middle.block
  ret void
}

!0 = distinct !{!0, !1, !2}
!1 = !{!"llvm.loop.isvectorized", i32 1}
!2 = !{!"llvm.loop.unroll.runtime.disable"}

define void @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:
  call void @pr128062(ptr %dst.start, i8 1, i16 2)
  ret void
}
